---
title: "Talk Title"
subtitle: "Subtitle / Paper Tagline"
author: "Tiago Ventura, Coauthor One, Coauthor Two"
date: "2026-01-01"
date-format: "[Venue Name --] YYYY"
execute:
  echo: false
  error: true
format:
  revealjs:
    transition: slide
    background-transition: fade
    code-line-numbers: false
    width: 1200
    height: 900
    center: true
    slide-number: true
    incremental: true
    chalkboard:
      buttons: false
    preview-links: auto
    footer: "Venue / Conference Name"
    theme: [simple, custom.scss]
---

## Top-line motivation goes here {.center}

Open with the puzzle or empirical hook. One short paragraph, then a few punchy bullets.

- Bullet one
- Bullet two
- **Bullet three -- the headline fact**

##

A second motivation slide if you need to set up the gap in the literature. Cite work inline (Author Year).

- Key prior study one
- Key prior study two

##

::: {.center .larger-font}

**RQ:** State the research question in one sentence.

:::

# Section: Context {background-color="#23395b"}

## Section header slide with assertion as the title

- Background point one
- Background point two
- Background point three

## Theory: name your mechanism here {.center}

State the mechanism in one sentence at the top of the slide.

- **Mechanism component A:** one-line description
- **Mechanism component B:** one-line description
- **Prediction:** what we should observe if the theory is right

# Data & Design {background-color="#23395b"}

## One-line description of the data {.center}

:::: {.columns}

::: {.column width="50%"}

**Source A**

- Bullet
- Bullet
- Bullet

:::

::: {.column width="50%"}

**Source B**

- Bullet
- Bullet
- Bullet

:::

::::

## Method in plain language {.center}

:::: {.columns}

::: {.column width="55%"}

- What you did, step by step
- Sample / filtering rules
- Identification strategy

:::

::: {.column width="45%"}

::: {.fragment}
**Intuition:** one or two sentences explaining why this works.
:::

:::

::::

# Results {background-color="#23395b"}

## Headline result as the slide title {.center}

![](output/figure_one.png){fig-align="center" width="85%"}

::: aside
[One-line caption with the key number.]{.midgray}
:::

## Second result, again with the punchline as the title {.center}

![](output/figure_two.png){fig-align="center" width="85%"}

::: aside
[Caption.]{.midgray}
:::

## Model spec slide (only if needed)

$$y_{ij} \sim \text{Distribution}(\theta_{ij})$$

$$\theta_{ij} = f(\alpha_i + \tau_j + \beta \cdot X_{ij})$$

::: {.fragment}

Where:

- $y_{ij}$ — outcome
- $\alpha_i$ — unit fixed effects
- $\tau_j$ — time fixed effects
- $\beta$ — **parameter of interest**

:::

# Discussion {background-color="#23395b"}

## Headline takeaway

::: {.nonincremental}

1. Takeaway one
2. Takeaway two
3. Takeaway three
4. Takeaway four

:::

## Implications

1. Implication one
2. Implication two
3. Implication three

## Limitations and next steps {.center}

::: {.nonincremental}

- Limitation one
- Limitation two
- Scope condition
- Next step

:::

## {background-color="#23395b"}

<br>

::: {.center .larger-font}
Thank you
:::

<br>

[tiago.ventura@georgetown.edu]{style="color: white; font-size: 0.8em;"}
